description_long: This is a weathered, cylindrical metal barrel with a distressed olive-green paint finish, showing significant surface wear, rust patches, and scratches. The barrel features two prominent circumferential reinforcement bands dividing its body into three sections. The ends of the barrel are sealed, with one end displaying a cracked, textured surface and the other end appearing smoother but still worn. The surface is uneven, with visible dents and areas of discoloration, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. There are no visible handles, lids, or labeling, and the object appears solid and robust. The overall appearance suggests it is an industrial or utility barrel, likely used for storage or transport of materials in urban or industrial environments.
description: A weathered, olive-green metal barrel with rust patches, dents, and two reinforcement bands. Both ends are sealed, with one end showing a cracked texture.
description_view_0: The end face of the barrel is visible, showing a cracked, textured surface with worn paint and rust.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the full cylindrical body, two reinforcement bands, and extensive surface wear with rust and discoloration.
description_view_2: The opposite end face is shown, smoother than the other end but still worn and scratched, with less pronounced cracking.
description_view_3: Another side view, highlighting a large dark patch of rust and the same reinforcement bands, with visible surface abrasions.
